CPC What skill did you cover.value: Icebreakers and Getting to Know each other | Communications | Relationships | Planning | Presentations with Powerpoint | Tips for Project Planning | We went through an overview of our next sessions -- no focus on skills today. | Getting to know each other, identified what the mentees want to learn | Business Q&A | Relationship & rapport building | Communication | Project PLanning & Analysis

CPC What component skill did you cover.value: Icebreakers | Effective Follow Up | Stakeholder Management | Decision making approach to college classes | Presentations Best Practice | SWOT Analysis | n/a - see above. | Business Q&A | rapport | Digital Body Language | SWOT Analysis

CPC Discussion topics.value: How to introduce yourselves to knew people; Carl is interested in Maths & Science; Shika is interested in nursing so we will take a broader approach to the sessions to work on our skills | Talked about the importance of "follow up" to build relationships, trust, and drive accountabilities with conversations you are having. Shikha and Carl gave good examples of when they do this now. | We talked about the application of Stakeholder Management across personal and professional projects; completed an exercise together to map an example project. We also spoke about the recent attacks on Asian Americans in Atlanta - both Shikha and Carl were concerned about the how Covid has driven this increased prejudice | Decision making in the context of college classes - possible classes for nursing & computer science. The value of learning languages. We discussed the support for the Asian community in the context of the recent events in Atlanta. | 6 best practice tips for PowerPoint presentations including: power of 3, tools, preparation skills | We talked about how all the lessons about stakeholder maps, time management and powerpoint were tied together and project planning was a good theme. We also talked about their school vacation and plans to prepare to go back to school | We did introductions. I asked them to describe their interests and their background and described my background. I also went through the topics and skills that I'd teach them in our upcoming sessions and asked them to identify which skills they were most interested in learning about. | Introductions, what they want to learn from the series (EG. Prioritization, time management, project planning) | Journey from college to where I am in my career today; maths skills required for my job, what requirements needed in the interview process | Career interest in pyschology | Zoom Etiquette, Email vs, Instant Messaging | Approaching projects, tools and methodologies

CPC Why.value: First session was focused on getting to know each other - will revise the guide following their asks | We are still finding our feet with the session format, feel like we will grow into it as the weeks progress | Good interaction, getting more used to talking to eachother | We had a good conversation about how to approach choosing classes for college - Including: decision making, time management, vocational vs. interest topics, having fun. | Great interaction from Shikha and Carl. | Shikha and Carl were super engaging we had a great conversation. | Tech issues made it difficult to communicate and be heard. I tried to dial in but it re-routed me to the main room. | Difficult audio, and the mentees were rather shy. Looking forward to getting to know them better. Chalked this up to day 1 | The audio and video is an ongoing challenge. Very hard to hear Jennyfer & Mariama. Started using Zoom chat instead. Pivot the session to a Q&A with the chat due to audio issues. | 5/5 session with Jennyfer building relationship. Unfortunately Cristian was unable to attend and Mariam joined later. It is hard to discuss certain skills the whole group is looking for with disjointed attendance. | Struggling with the audio & video | More interactive!
